toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run tests with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. 

For .NET, crash dumps can be collected using the `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` environment variable. When set to `1`, the runtime automatically generates a mini dump when the process crashes.

Key environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` - enables mini dump collection
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` - full dump (1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full)


- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=/path/to/dump` - specifies where to write the dump

Actually, there's a simpler approach with the `--blame-crash` flag in `dotnet test`, which is specifically designed for this and automatically handles crash dump collection. That's the most straightforward way to go.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-aa354f0921684b86b32534874f404eec && dotnet test --blame-crash 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. The command to run tests with crash dump collection is:

```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

The `--blame-crash` flag enables the blame data collector in crash-dump mode. If any test crashes the host process, a full crash dump will be written to the `TestResults/` directory alongside a sequence file showing which test was running at the time. No additional packages or configuration are needed — it's built into `dotnet test`.
